title: Constipation
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives unless reuse terms are confirmed for the exact content.
supports: Constipation may involve fewer than three bowel movements per week, hard or lumpy stools, painful or difficult stool passage, or incomplete emptying.
Constipation that does not improve with self-care or is long-term should be discussed with a clinician.
Bleeding from the rectum, blood in stool, continual abdominal pain, or other medical-problem signs should prompt medical evaluation.
Treatment and prevention may include dietary changes, activity, over-the-counter medicines, prescription medicines, biofeedback, or surgery depending on cause and response.
